page: Microsoft Azure D2d_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDv4 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The configuration includes 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled, yielding 2 virtual CPUs (vCPUs). The processor is equipped with 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 35 MB of L3 cache, alongside support for AVX-512 and AES instructions. It is provisioned with 8.0 GB of system memory and includes 77 GB of local storage.

Benchmark data indicates average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1404.68 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1173.0. However, multi-core and memory-bound metrics fall into lower percentiles. The stress-ng multi-core score is 1846.98, and the Geekbench multi-core score is 1440.0. Memory bandwidth is limited, measuring 12.21 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 8.99 GB/sec for write operations. Database and transactional performance are also constrained, with Redis SET operations reaching 1.75 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 1.95 GB/sec.

Given the dedicated CPU allocation and balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the D2d_v4 offers a predictable entry-level hardware profile. The inclusion of 77 GB of local storage provides a resource density advantage for workloads requiring local scratch space without relying solely on network-attached storage. Due to its moderate single-core capabilities and lower multi-core and memory bandwidth performance, this server is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, single-threaded utility scripts, and development or testing environments that do not demand high parallel processing or intensive memory throughput.
description: Microsoft Azure D2d_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DDv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz, supported by 8.0 GB of RAM and 77 GB of local storage. Performance benchmarks show average single-core execution, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1404.68 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1173.0. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are relatively low, with large block read bandwidth at 12.21 GB/sec and a multi-core Geekbench score of 1440.0. This server provides a balanced, entry-level resource profile with local storage. It is best suited for lightweight general-purpose applications, development environments, and basic web serving tasks that do not require high parallel processing power.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine with dedicated Intel Xeon processing and local storage for entry-level workloads.

Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and 77 GB of local storage.
Delivers average single-core performance suitable for basic sequential tasks.
